# Simulation Labs

> Behavioral simulation for real user flows. We run pre-trained computer-use agents, mechanically degraded and ICP-calibrated, against a live flow and record the exact pixel where each one abandons. We simulate what users do, not what they say.

## What it does
- capability: behavioral_simulation(target_url, task, persona_ids[])
- persona: pre-trained computer-use agent (screenshot-in / action-out); consumed, not trained
- degradation: perception{gaussian_blur, downsample, colour_transform}; actuation{coordinate_noise sigma_px, tremor}; budget{max_steps, wall_clock_ms}; viewport{w,h}
- calibration: persona distribution tuned to customer ICP segments (config, not gradient training)
- loop: screenshot -> perceive(perturb) -> model.action -> actuate(jitter) -> execute(playwright) -> emit(event)
- coords: model emits 0..1000 normalized; denormalized to true viewport px; executed verbatim
- output: survival_curve, abandonment_heatmap, per_persona_stats, score[0..100], video_receipts, cloned_voice_exit_interviews
- semantics: rank-order signal / evidence-of-risk; NOT a forecast, NOT a conformance verdict

## Evidence
- prompted-persona fidelity ~11.86% (arXiv:2503.20749)
- tremor sigma=14px miss-rate: 60.9%@24px, 20.5%@44px, <5%@>=62px (offline Monte-Carlo, 20k trials/cell, illustrative)
- 173 tests; MIT-licensed; frozen-contract 5-module architecture; built at the H Company Computer Use Hackathon

## Stack
- Holo (holo3-1-35b-a3b), Playwright, Gradium (voice), Claude (optional narration), NVIDIA NemoClaw (network policy)

## Triggers
- manual, mcp_skill (mid-session), ci_cd_gate (regression vs last-passing)

## Contact
- Apply for design-partner access: satyam@agentmade.ai
- Repo: https://github.com/SatyamDave/simulation-labs
